a.label: Push hard for the one nobody's seen

prompt: A character is crying in the rain at the emotional climax. You're more moved when the film is…

b.label: Self-aware about the cliché it's invoking

prompt: Sunday afternoon. Rain. One of these is on.
Which frame would you rather live inside for two hours?

still.description: A perfectly centered hotel lobby in cotton-candy pink, bellhop frozen mid-step under a clock with brass hands, dollhouse symmetry.
A cramped jewelry counter under buzzing fluorescents, faces in close-up shouting over each other, the camera trembling like it's also panicking.

prompt: A friend says: 'Trust me, the first 40 minutes are slow but it's worth it.'

a.label: In.
a.subtitle: Patience is part of the deal

prompt: You want to feel something tonight. Which kind of feeling?
On your watchlist right now. Pick the one you'll *actually* press play on this week.

b.label: It's a hurdle.
b.subtitle: I want to look at the screen

prompt: A long, beautiful, doomed romance. Which one are you in for?
You finish a movie at 11pm Tuesday alone. What did it just do to you?

a.label: Left me staring at the ceiling
a.subtitle: The good kind of haunted

prompt: Movie's been going 35 minutes. You're not bored, but nothing's *happened* yet. Are you…
Same director-ish vibe, different temperatures. Pick.

still.description: A kitchen lit by the last hour of daylight, dust in the air, a mug of tea on a scratched wooden table — the whole room the color of toast.
A high-rise hallway at 3am, fluorescent strips reflecting on wet concrete, a figure in silhouette at the far end — everything the color of a dropped knife.

prompt: You're traveling alone. Hotel. Laptop. Pick one.
The premise has dragons / time travel / a multiverse. You:
Optional. Name a TV show you loved enough to recommend twice.
